title: [release-1.39] Bump runc to v1.2.8 - CVE-2025-52881
body:
Bump runc to v1.2.8 to fix CVE-2025-52881.  This also
fixes CVE-2025-31133 and CVE-2025-52565

Then bump Buildah to v1.39.5

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-126631, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-126924

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Try `go get golang.org/x/tools@v0.26.0 && go mod tidy && go mod vendor` in `tests/tools`.
@nalind I've added a new commit with the tools bump.  I'd love to know how you came to that conclusion....

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5*, *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/release-1.39/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat,lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> @nalind I've added a new commit with the tools bump. I'd love to know how you came to that conclusion....

Nothing particularly clever - the compiler error was from that module, and it wasn't the latest one (they tend to bump at least for new compiler versions), so I started bumping its version until the error stopped happening.

/lgtm
